questionText: Show annual revenue, total cost, gross profit, gross margin percentage, and total order lines by fiscal year. Return columns: Fiscal Year, total_revenue, total_cost, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct, total_order_lines. Sort by Fiscal Year ascending.
questionLabel: [Q1][medium] Show annual revenue, total cost, gross profit, gross margin percentage, and total order lines by fiscal year. Return columns: Fiscal Year, total_revenue, total_cost, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct, total_order_lines. Sort by Fiscal Year ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "SELECT d.Fiscal Year AS FiscalYear,
       SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS total_revenue,
       SUM(s.Total Product Cost) AS total_cost,
       SUM(s.Extended Amount) - SUM(s.Total Product Cost) AS gross_profit,
       CASE WHEN SUM(s.Total Product Cost) = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE (SUM(s.Extended Amount) - SUM(s.Total Product Cost)) * 100.0 / SUM(s.Total Product Cost)
       END AS gross_margin_pct,
       COUNT(s.SalesOrderLineKey) AS total_order_lines
FROM Sales s
JOIN Date d ON s.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
GROUP BY d.Fiscal Year
ORDER BY d.Fiscal Year ASC;"}

function.name: run_sql_query
function.description: Execute a SQL query against the DuckDB database. Call this when you have a SQL query ready to run.

questionText: Compare the Reseller and Internet sales channels showing total revenue, revenue share percentage, number of distinct sales orders, order lines, average line amount, and gross margin percentage. Return columns: Channel, total_revenue, revenue_share_pct, total_orders, order_lines, avg_line_amount, gross_margin_pct. Sort by total_revenue descending.
questionLabel: [Q2][hard] Compare the Reseller and Internet sales channels showing total revenue, revenue share percentage, number of distinct sales orders, order lines, average line amount, and gross margin percentage. Return columns: Channel, total_revenue, revenue_share_pct, total_orders, order_lines, avg_line_amount, gross_margin_pct. Sort by total_revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "WITH reseller_sales AS (
  SELECT 'Reseller' AS Channel,
         SUM(Sales.Amount) AS total_revenue,
         COUNT(DISTINCT SalesOrderLineKey) AS total_orders,
         COUNT(*) AS order_lines,
         AVG(Sales.Amount) AS avg_line_amount,
         (SUM(Sales.Amount) / NULLIF(SUM(SUM(Sales.Amount)) OVER (), 0)) * 100 AS gross_margin_pct
  FROM Sales
  JOIN Sales_Order ON Sales.SalesOrderLineKey = Sales_Order.SalesOrderLineKey
  WHERE Sales.Channel = 'Reseller'
),
internet_sales AS (
  SELECT 'Internet' AS Channel,
         SUM(Sales.Amount) AS total_revenue,
         COUNT(DISTINCT SalesOrderLineKey) AS total_orders,
         COUNT(*) AS order_lines,
         AVG(Sales.Amount) AS avg_line_amount,
         (SUM(Sales.Amount) / NULLIF(SUM(SUM(Sales.Amount)) OVER (), 0)) * 100 AS gross_margin_pct
  FROM Sales
  WHERE Sales.Channel = 'Internet'
)
SELECT Channel,
       total_revenue,

questionText: Show revenue, revenue share percentage, units sold, gross profit, and gross margin percentage by product category. Return columns: Category, revenue, revenue_share_pct, units_sold, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct. Sort by revenue descending.
questionLabel: [Q3][hard] Show revenue, revenue share percentage, units sold, gross profit, and gross margin percentage by product category. Return columns: Category, revenue, revenue_share_pct, units_sold, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct. Sort by revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "SELECT p.Category,
       SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue,
       ROUND(SUM(s.Extended Amount) / SUM(SUM(s.Extended Amount)) OVER (), 4) * 100 AS revenue_share_pct,
       SUM(s.Order Quantity) AS units_sold,
       SUM(s.Extended Amount) - SUM(s.Total Product Cost) AS gross_profit,
       ROUND((SUM(s.Extended Amount) - SUM(s.Total Product Cost)) / SUM(s.Extended Amount) * 100, 2) AS gross_margin_pct
FROM Sales s
JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
GROUP BY p.Category
ORDER BY revenue DESC;"}

questionText: Show revenue and units sold broken down by territory group, country, region, and fiscal year. Return columns: Group, Country, Region, Fiscal Year, revenue, units_sold. Sort by Group, Country, Region, then Fiscal Year all ascending.
questionLabel: [Q5][hard] Show revenue and units sold broken down by territory group, country, region, and fiscal year. Return columns: Group, Country, Region, Fiscal Year, revenue, units_sold. Sort by Group, Country, Region, then Fiscal Year all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "SELECT
    st.Group,
    st.Country,
    st.Region,
    d.Fiscal Year,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue,
    SUM(s.Order Quantity) AS units_sold
FROM Sales s
JOIN Sales_Territory st ON s.SalesTerritoryKey = st.SalesTerritoryKey
JOIN Date d ON s.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
GROUP BY
    st.Group,
    st.Country,
    st.Region,
    d.Fiscal Year
ORDER BY
    st.Group ASC,
    st.Country ASC,
    st.Region ASC,
    d.Fiscal Year ASC;"}

Show revenue and units sold broken down by territory group, country, region, and fiscal year. Return columns: Group, Country, Region, Fiscal Year, revenue, units_sold. Sort by Group, Country, Region, then Fiscal Year all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"SELECT
    st.Group,
    st.Country,
    st.Region,
    d.Fiscal Year,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue,
    SUM(s.Order Quantity) AS units_sold
FROM Sales s
JOIN Sales_Territory st ON s.SalesTerritoryKey = st.SalesTerritoryKey
JOIN Date d ON s.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
GROUP BY
    st.Group,
    st.Country,
    st.Region,
    d.Fiscal Year
ORDER BY
    st.Group ASC,
    st.Country ASC,
    st.Region ASC,
    d.Fiscal Year ASC;"}

questionLabel: [Q6][hard] List the top 15 direct customers (excluding CustomerKey -1) by lifetime revenue, showing customer name, city, state/province, country/region, total orders, total line items, total units, lifetime revenue, and lifetime profit. Return columns: Customer, City, State-Province, Country-Region, total_orders, total_line_items, total_units, lifetime_revenue, lifetime_profit. Sort by lifetime_revenue descending, limit to 15 rows.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments: {"sql": "SELECT c.Customer AS Customer, c.City AS City, c.State_Province AS State-Province, c.Country_Region AS Country-Region, COUNT(DISTINCT s.OrderDateKey) AS total_orders, COUNT(s.SalesOrderLineKey) AS total_line_items, SUM(s.Order Quantity) AS total_units, SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS lifetime_revenue, SUM(s.Extended Amount - s.Total Product Cost) AS lifetime_profit FROM Customer c LEFT JOIN Sales s ON c.CustomerKey = s.CustomerKey WHERE c.CustomerKey <> -1 GROUP BY c.CustomerKey, c.Customer, c.City, c.State_Province, c.Country_Region ORDER BY lifetime_revenue DESC LIMIT 15;"}

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "SELECT
    r.Business_Type,
    COUNT(DISTINCT r.ResellerKey) AS reseller_count,

questionText: Show order lines, revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the subcategory, gross profit, and margin percentage for each product subcategory. questionLabel: [Q9][hard] Show order lines, revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the subcategory, gross profit, and margin percentage for each product subcategory. Return columns: Subcategory, order_lines, revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "WITH line_items AS (
  SELECT
    p.Subcategory,
    COUNT(*) AS order_lines,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue,
    SUM(s.Order Quantity) AS units_sold
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
  GROUP BY p.Subcategory
),
list_price AS (
  SELECT
    p.Subcategory,
    AVG(p.List Price) AS avg_list_price
  FROM Product p
  GROUP BY p.Subcategory
),
profit AS (
  SELECT
    p.Subcategory,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount - s.Total Product Cost) AS gross_profit
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
  GROUP BY p.Subcategory
)
SELECT
  li.Subcategory,
  li.order_lines,
  li.revenue,
  li.units_sold,
  CASE WHEN li.units_sold = 0 THEN NULL ELSE li.revenue / li.units_sold END AS revenue_per_unit,
  lp.avg_list_price,
  COALESCE(p.gross_profit,0) AS gross_profit,
  CASE WHEN li.units_sold = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (COALESCE(p.gross_profit,0) / li.revenue) * 100 END AS margin_pct
FROM line_items li
LEFT JOIN list_price lp ON li.Subcategory = lp.Subcategory
LEFT JOIN profit p ON li.Subcategory = p.Subcategory
ORDER BY li.revenue DESC;"}

questionText: Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.
questionLabel: [Q10][hard] Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "WITH yearly_rev AS (
  SELECT
    s.Channel,
    p.Category,
    d.Fiscal Year,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Sales_Order so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
  JOIN Date d ON s.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
  JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
  GROUP BY s.Channel, p.Category, d.Fiscal Year
)
SELECT
  Channel,
  Category,
  Fiscal Year AS prior_year,
  LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Year) AS prior_revenue,
  revenue AS current_year,
  revenue AS current_revenue,
  CASE
    WHEN LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Year) IS NULL THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Year)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Year)) * 100, 2)
  END AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM yearly_rev
ORDER BY Channel, Category, prior_year ASC;"}

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"WITH yearly_rev AS (
  SELECT
    s.Channel,
    p.Category,
    d.Fiscal Year,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Sales_Order so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
  JOIN Date d ON s.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey

  GROUP BY s.Channel, p.Category, d.Fiscal Year
)

  Channel,
  Category,
  Fiscal Year AS prior_year,

  revenue AS current_revenue,

    WHEN LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Year) IS NULL THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Year)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Year)) * 100, 2)
  END AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM yearly_rev
ORDER BY Channel, Category, prior_year ASC;"}

Return columns: Channel, Category, total_revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, realized_discount_pct, gross_profit, margin_pct. function.name: run_sql_query
function.description: Execute a SQL query against the DuckDB database. Call this when you have a SQL query ready to run.

questionText: Show total revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the category, realized discount percentage versus that average list price, gross profit, and margin percentage by sales channel and product category. Return columns: Channel, Category, total_revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, realized_discount_pct, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by Channel ascending, then total_revenue descending.
questionLabel: [Q21][hard] Show total revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the category, realized discount percentage versus that average list price, gross profit, and margin percentage by sales channel and product category. Return columns: Channel, Category, total_revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, realized_discount_pct, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by Channel ascending, then total_revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "WITH sales_agg AS (
  SELECT
    s.Channel,
    p.Category,
    SUM(s.ExtendedAmount) AS total_revenue,
    SUM(s.OrderQuantity) AS units_sold,
    SUM(s.ExtendedAmount) / NULLIF(SUM(s.OrderQuantity),0) AS revenue_per_unit,
    AVG(p.ListPrice) AS avg_list_price,
    SUM(s.ExtendedAmount) - SUM(s.TotalProductCost) AS gross_profit,
    CASE WHEN SUM(s.OrderQuantity) = 0 THEN NULL
         ELSE (SUM(s.ExtendedAmount) - SUM(s.TotalProductCost)) / SUM(s.OrderQuantity) END AS realized_discount_pct,
    p.Category,
    s.Channel
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Sales_Order so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
  JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
  GROUP BY s.Channel, p.Category, s.ExtendedAmount, s.TotalProductCost
)
SELECT
  Channel,
  Category,
  total_revenue,
  units_sold,
  revenue_per_unit,
  avg_list_price,
  realized_discount_pct,
  gross_profit,
  CASE WHEN revenue_per_unit = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (gross_profit / revenue_per_unit) END AS margin_pct
FROM sales_agg
ORDER BY Channel ASC, total_revenue DESC;"}

Show total revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the category, realized discount percentage versus that average list price, gross profit, and margin percentage by sales channel and product category. Return columns: Channel, Category, total_revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, realized_discount_pct, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by Channel ascending, then total_revenue descending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"WITH sales_agg AS (
  SELECT
    s.Channel,
    p.Category,
    SUM(s.ExtendedAmount) AS total_revenue,
    SUM(s.OrderQuantity) AS units_sold,
    SUM(s.ExtendedAmount) / NULLIF(SUM(s.OrderQuantity),0) AS revenue_per_unit,
    AVG(p.ListPrice) AS avg_list_price,
    SUM(s.ExtendedAmount) - SUM(s.TotalProductCost) AS gross_profit,
    CASE WHEN SUM(s.OrderQuantity) = 0 THEN NULL
         ELSE (SUM(s.ExtendedAmount) - SUM(s.TotalProductCost)) / SUM(s.OrderQuantity) END AS realized_discount_pct,
    p.Category,
    s.Channel
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Sales_Order so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
  JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
  GROUP BY s.Channel, p.Category, s.ExtendedAmount, s.TotalProductCost
)
SELECT
  Channel,
  Category,
  total_revenue,
  units_sold,
  revenue_per_unit,
  avg_list_price,
  realized_discount_pct,
  gross_profit,
  CASE WHEN revenue_per_unit = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (gross_profit / revenue_per_unit) END AS margin_pct
FROM sales_agg
ORDER BY Channel ASC, total_revenue DESC;"}

questionText: Show quarter-over-quarter revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal quarters. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_quarter, prior_revenue, current_quarter, current_revenue, qoq_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_quarter all ascending.
questionLabel: [Q22][hard] Show quarter-over-quarter revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal quarters. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_quarter, prior_revenue, current_quarter, current_revenue, qoq_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_quarter all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "WITH quarterly_sales AS (
  SELECT
    s.Channel,
    p.Category,
    d.Fiscal Quarter,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Sales_Order so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
  JOIN Date d ON s.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
  JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
  GROUP BY s.Channel, p.Category, d.Fiscal Quarter
)
SELECT
  Channel,
  Category,
  Fiscal Quarter AS prior_quarter,
  LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Quarter) AS prior_revenue,
  revenue AS current_quarter,
  revenue AS current_revenue,
  CASE
    WHEN LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Quarter) IS NULL THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Quarter)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Quarter)) * 100, 2)
  END AS qoq_growth_pct
FROM quarterly_sales
ORDER BY Channel, Category, prior_quarter ASC;"}

Show quarter-over-quarter revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal quarters. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_quarter, prior_revenue, current_quarter, current_revenue, qoq_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_quarter all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql":"WITH quarterly_sales AS (
  SELECT
    s.Channel,
    p.Category,
    d.Fiscal Quarter,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Sales_Order so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
  JOIN Date d ON s.OrderDateKey = d.DateKey
  JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
  GROUP BY s.Channel, p.Category, d.Fiscal Quarter
)
SELECT
  Channel,
  Category,
  Fiscal Quarter AS prior_quarter,
  LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Quarter) AS prior_revenue,
  revenue AS current_quarter,
  revenue AS current_revenue,
  CASE
    WHEN LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Quarter) IS NULL THEN NULL
    ELSE ROUND(((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Quarter)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY Channel, Category ORDER BY Fiscal Quarter)) * 100, 2)
  END AS qoq_growth_pct
FROM quarterly_sales
ORDER BY Channel, Category, prior_quarter ASC;"}

questionText: For each product category, list the top 2 subcategories by revenue, showing revenue rank within the category, revenue, units sold, gross profit, and revenue share percentage within the category. Return columns: Category, revenue_rank, Subcategory, revenue, units_sold, gross_profit, category_revenue_share_pct. Sort by Category, revenue_rank, then Subcategory all ascending.
questionLabel: [Q23][hard] For each product category, list the top 2 subcategories by revenue, showing revenue rank within the category, revenue, units sold, gross profit, and revenue share percentage within the category. Return columns: Category, revenue_rank, Subcategory, revenue, units_sold, gross_profit, category_revenue_share_pct. Sort by Category, revenue_rank, then Subcategory all ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "WITH category_rev AS (
  SELECT
    p.Category,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS category_revenue,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) * SUM(s.Unit Price Discount Pct) / SUM(s.Unit Price Discount Pct) AS category_gross_profit,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) * SUM(s.Unit Price Discount Pct) / SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS category_revenue_share_pct,
    COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY p.Category) AS cat_cnt
  FROM Product p
  JOIN Sales s ON p.ProductKey = s.ProductKey
  GROUP BY p.Category
),
sub_rev AS (
  SELECT
    p.Category,
    p.Subcategory,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue,
    SUM(s.Order Quantity) AS units_sold,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) * SUM(s.Unit Price Discount Pct) / SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS gross_profit,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.Category ORDER BY SUM(s.Extended Amount) DESC) AS revenue_rank
  FROM Product p
  JOIN Sales s ON p.ProductKey = s.ProductKey
  GROUP BY p.Category, p.Subcategory
)
SELECT
  cr.Category,
  sr.revenue_rank,
  sr.Subcategory,
  sr.revenue,
  sr.units_sold,
  sr.gross_profit,
  cr.category_revenue_share_pct
FROM sub_rev sr
JOIN category_rev cr ON sr.Category = cr.Category
WHERE sr.revenue_rank <= 2
ORDER BY cr.Category ASC, sr.revenue_rank ASC, sr.Subcategory ASC;"}

sub_rev AS (
  SELECT
    p.Category,
    p.Subcategory,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue,
    SUM(s.Order Quantity) AS units_sold,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) * SUM(s.Unit Price Discount Pct) / SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS gross_profit,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY p.Category ORDER BY SUM(s.Extended Amount) DESC) AS revenue_rank
  FROM Product p
  JOIN Sales s ON p.ProductKey = s.ProductKey
  GROUP BY p.Category, p.Subcategory
)
SELECT
  cr.Category,
  sr.revenue_rank,
  sr.Subcategory,
  sr.revenue,
  sr.units_sold,
  sr.gross_profit,
  cr.category_revenue_share_pct
FROM sub_rev sr
JOIN category_rev cr ON sr.Category = cr.Category
WHERE sr.revenue_rank <= 2
ORDER BY cr.Category ASC, sr.revenue_rank ASC, sr.Subcategory ASC;"}

questionLabel: [Q24][hard] Within each sales channel, show revenue, revenue share percentage, cumulative revenue share percentage ordered by revenue descending, number of distinct sales orders, and gross profit by product category. Return columns: Channel, Category, revenue, channel_revenue_share_pct, cumulative_channel_revenue_share_pct, orders, gross_profit. Sort by Channel ascending, then revenue descending, then Category ascending.

function.name: run_sql_query
function.arguments:
{"sql": "WITH channel_category AS (
  SELECT
    s.Channel,
    p.Category,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS revenue,
    COUNT(DISTINCT s.SalesOrderLineKey) AS orders,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) * 100.0 / SUM(SUM(s.Extended Amount)) OVER (PARTITION BY s.Channel) AS channel_revenue_share_pct,
    SUM(s.Extended Amount) * 100.0 / SUM(SUM(s.Extended Amount)) OVER () AS cumulative_channel_revenue_share_pct
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Sales_Order so ON s.SalesOrderLineKey = so.SalesOrderLineKey
  JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
  GROUP BY s.Channel, p.Category
), channel_total AS (
  SELECT s.Channel, SUM(s.Extended Amount) AS total_rev FROM Sales s GROUP BY s.Channel
)
SELECT
  cc.Channel,
  cc.Category,
  cc.revenue,
  cc.channel_revenue_share_pct,
  cc.cumulative_channel_revenue_share_pct,
  cc.orders,
  cc.revenue * (gp.gross_profit_total / ct.total_rev) AS gross_profit
FROM channel_category cc
JOIN channel_total ct ON cc.Channel = ct.Channel
JOIN (
  SELECT p.Category, SUM(s.Extended Amount - s.Total Product Cost) AS gross_profit_total
  FROM Sales s
  JOIN Product p ON s.ProductKey = p.ProductKey
  GROUP BY p.Category
) gp ON cc.Category = gp.Category
ORDER BY cc.Channel ASC, cc.revenue DESC, cc.Category ASC;"}
